blocks.text: DTC P15C5: Master Power negative pressure sensor A characteristic abnormal (Lo side)

The PGM-FI ECU calculates the negative pressure in the master power based on the output voltage of the master power negative pressure sensor and determines whether idling stop is permitted. When the master power negative pressure sensor remains below a predetermined value for a predetermined period of time or longer, the PGM-FI ECU counts as one count, and when the predetermined number of counts is reached, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows: *: Idling stop system warning light (orange) illuminates D/C: Drive Cycle

blocks.text: When the master power negative pressure sensor value is less than -4kPa (-35mmHg) for 2.1 seconds, it is counted as 1 count, and the condition continues for 5 times.

items.text: Master Power negative pressure sensor failure

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the idling stop system warning light (orange) will illuminate without the brake warning light (orange) and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data will be stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.

If no vehicle malfunction is detected in the next ignition ON mode, the Idle Stop System warning light (orange) will turn off. The temporary DTCs, DTCs and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by disconnecting the 12V battery terminal.
